skill.name: upgrading-expo
skill.description: Upgrade Expo SDK versions safely with automated dependency management and breaking change handling
skill.summary: Safely upgrade Expo SDK versions while handling breaking changes, dependencies, and configuration updates
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 14 patterns but all are false positives. The skill is a markdown documentation file containing instructional bash commands in code blocks (not executed), reference URLs (not network requests), and no actual code execution capability. Safe for publication.

content.value_statement: Expo SDK upgrades often break apps due to dependency conflicts and API changes. This skill guides you through safe upgrades with proper testing and migration steps.
actual_capabilities: Guide through Expo SDK version upgrade process step by step
Identify and handle breaking changes between SDK versions
Update Expo packages using expo install commands
Migrate app.json configuration for new SDK requirements
Verify upgrade success using expo-doctor diagnostics
limitations: Cannot automatically execute upgrade commands - user must run commands manually
Cannot test the actual app - user must perform testing on their devices
Does not handle custom native modules that may require manual updates
Cannot resolve third-party package incompatibilities automatically

use_cases.title: Annual SDK Upgrade
use_cases.description: Upgrade your Expo app to the latest SDK version to access new features and security patches
use_cases.target_user: Mobile developers maintaining production Expo apps

use_cases.title: Breaking Change Migration
use_cases.description: Navigate major version upgrades with deprecated API migration and configuration changes
use_cases.target_user: Teams upgrading across multiple SDK versions

use_cases.title: Pre-Launch Compatibility Check
use_cases.description: Ensure app dependencies are compatible before releasing to app stores
use_cases.target_user: Developers preparing apps for iOS and Android store submission

prompt_templates.title: Basic SDK Upgrade
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to upgrade my Expo app from SDK 49 to SDK 50. What are the steps?
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple version upgrade with minimal breaking changes

prompt_templates.title: Breaking Changes Review
prompt_templates.prompt: What breaking changes should I expect when upgrading from Expo SDK 48 to 50? List the affected APIs and migration steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-version upgrade requiring careful migration planning

prompt_templates.title: Dependency Conflict Resolution
prompt_templates.prompt: After running expo install --fix, I have peer dependency warnings for react-native-reanimated. How do I resolve this?
prompt_templates.scenario: Troubleshooting package compatibility issues during upgrade

prompt_templates.title: Full Upgrade with Testing Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete upgrade plan for moving from Expo SDK 49 to 51 including: pre-upgrade checklist, command sequence, configuration changes, and a testing checklist for iOS and Android.

output: 1. Create a backup branch: git checkout -b upgrade-expo-50
2. Update Expo CLI: npm install -g expo-cli@latest
3. Install Expo SDK 50: npx expo install expo@50
6. Test on iOS and Android simulators
- SplashScreen module now requires explicit imports
- StatusBar API moved to expo-status-bar package
- App auth flow updated in expo-auth-session
Migration steps provided for each change with code examples

best_practices: Always upgrade in a feature branch and commit before starting
Use npx expo install instead of npm install for Expo packages
Run expo-doctor before and after upgrade to verify configuration
anti_patterns: Using npm install instead of expo install for Expo packages
Merging upgrade changes without testing on both iOS and Android

faq.question: Can I skip SDK versions when upgrading?
faq.answer: Yes, but upgrading multiple versions at once increases risk. For large jumps (2+ versions), consider incremental upgrades to catch breaking changes more easily.

faq.question: Do I need to eject from Expo to upgrade?
faq.answer: No. Expo managed workflow supports SDK upgrades without ejecting. Only custom native modules may require additional configuration.

faq.question: How long does an SDK upgrade typically take?
faq.answer: Simple upgrades take 30-60 minutes. Complex upgrades with breaking changes or custom native code may take several hours or require spreading work across multiple sessions.

faq.question: What should I do if the upgrade breaks my app?
faq.answer: Use git to revert to your backup branch. Review the error messages, check the Expo migration guide for your specific version, and address breaking changes one at a time.

faq.question: Do I need to update Xcode and Android Studio before upgrading?
faq.answer: Not necessarily, but having the latest versions helps. Some SDK versions require minimum Xcode or Android Studio versions - check the release notes.

faq.question: Can this skill upgrade my third-party packages automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill guides you through using expo install --fix which updates Expo packages. Third-party packages must be manually checked for compatibility with the new SDK version.
